_redaction_notice.rationale: Thread tokens and token-bearing URLs are redacted. Bot handles, PIDs, timestamps, transcript rows, owner-reply text, summaries, and validator output are retained as E2E evidence.

host.source: redacted parsed mandate from host owner context

guest.source: redacted parsed mandate from guest owner context

transcript.text: Hi! I'm reaching out on behalf of George at Zedlands to discuss a potential sponsorship collaboration. We'd love to explore a package that works for both sides — what's your best opening offer for a branded content or game sponsorship deal?
Hi! Thanks for reaching out. Tom's opening offer is ¥75,000 for 2 short branded videos, a 1-hour Bilibili livestream, and 45-day usage rights. Does that align with George's budget?
Tom's opening is ¥75,000 — ¥10,000 over your ceiling. I can counter at ¥65,000 or propose extra deliverables instead. Shall I offer ¥65,000 cash + bonus content, or do you want to stretch?
George's ceiling is ¥65,000 — slightly below the opening. Can you do the same deliverables (2 branded videos + 1-hour livestream) at ¥65,000, or extend usage rights to 60 days as a trade-off?
¥65,000 works if payment is 50% upfront and Tom gets approval rights on final edits. Can George confirm both? That closes the gap cleanly.
Tom accepted ¥65,000 with 50% upfront and approval on final edits — please confirm to close the deal.
Tom accepted ¥65,000 with 50% upfront and approval on final edits. Terms are within your ceiling. Please confirm "yes" to close, or reply with any changes.
George — Tom confirmed ¥65,000, 50% upfront, 50% on delivery, approval on final edits. Please reply "yes" to close or specify any changes needed.
George, Tom's deal is ¥65,000 (within your ¥65,000 ceiling) — 50% upfront, 50% on delivery, approval on final edits. Say "yes" to close or note any changes.

snapshot.goal: Negotiate a creator-brand sponsorship package. Host must not accept above the owner mandate. If an above-ceiling term appears, ASK_OWNER before continuing. Final terms must include price, deliverables, payment timing, usage rights, approval rights, and a concise owner-ready summary.

coverage_note: Failed T3 v0: bridge correctly emitted ASK_OWNER after an above-ceiling ¥75,000 proposal, but the mutating GET owner-reply fallback in the Telegram notification was consumed automatically with REPLACE_WITH_OWNER_DECISION placeholder text. This led to repeated bogus owner_reply events until the test room was manually closed.
review_notes: Failure source was protocol surface, not mandate detection: ASK_OWNER fired as expected.
Fix applied afterward: owner-reply is POST-only and bridge notifications no longer include a mutating GET URL.
This run also exposed that waiting_owner bridges must observe closed rooms before polling for owner replies.
